title: Opus vs Sonnet vs Haiku: when to use each

body.itemA: Claude Opus 4.6 — the most intelligent model. Best for building agents, complex coding, and tasks requiring deep reasoning. Highest cost, slowest speed.
body.itemB: Claude Sonnet 4.6 — the balanced model. Best combination of speed and intelligence. Good default for most production use cases.

dimensions.name: Best for
dimensions.a: Opus: complex agents, hard coding tasks, deep analysis
dimensions.b: Sonnet: production workloads needing speed + quality balance

dimensions.name: Trade-off
dimensions.a: Opus: highest quality, but slower and more expensive
dimensions.b: Sonnet: slightly less capable, but faster and cheaper

dimensions.name: When to pick
dimensions.a: Opus: accuracy matters more than latency or cost
dimensions.b: Sonnet: you need good results at reasonable speed/cost
